prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Oh, sweetheart, a CPU is like the little brain inside your computer! It’s the part that does all the thinking and figuring out, just like how Grandma decides what ingredients to use when baking cookies. When you click on something or type a letter, the CPU quickly reads your instructions and tells the rest of the computer what to do. It works *very* fast, but it can get a little warm, which is why computers sometimes have fans to keep it cool. Without the CPU, your computer wouldn’t know how to play games, show pictures, or even turn on! It’s a tiny but mighty helper, just like you!
